reviews.review_description:
(Note to the devs at the bottom.)

Let me preface my review with, 5 stars yes, but let me warn you before spend your money. If you do not like more simple games or repeating the same type of task (Like power washing simulator, thief simulator, or Farming simulator), YOU WILL NOT LIKE THIS GAME!! 

BUT! If that's not a problem, this might just be my favorite vr game of all time and it could be yours too! I’ll try to seperate my review into sections
(And YES there is multiplayer, but read on first)

Controls:
Some people have issues with the realism and difficulty of controlling the excavators, but I personally love it. If in the tutorial, you read what you are told and move slowly, you can easily master the movement. 

The gameplay:
There isn’t much to say that’s truly remarkable about the gameplay, but that’s the fun part. It makes you feel like you’re just an excavator driver. You dig trenches and holes, smash rocks with a jackhammer, saw trees down with a saw blade, trim hedges and foliage, and move cargo and junk with a claw. So NO! It’s not all digging!! It’s very peaceful to play when you want to waste time or just relax in some heavy machinery. The levels are also pretty funny (not in practice but in the reasons why you’re there).

Replayability:
This is spot on with three modes to choose from. Zen is easy, ungraded, with no mistakes affecting you. Graded is the normal, try to be careful but still simple mode. And Time Trial is a… well time trial. All three modes are available for every level I’ve seen so far in my play through. This lets you try every level at least 3 times in different ways, plus you can always go back to previous levels if you want to do them again.

Entertainment and World: 
I relate it to Powerwashing Simulator a lot based on how it works. You pick a job, start the excavator, finish the tasks specified, and you're done. Same thing as Powerwashing sim where you select a job, clean, and done. Yea, it can be a bit monotonous, but what do you expect? It’s a game about driving an excavator! Also, it has funny messages, world story, and thematic elements all around the place just the same way as Powerwashing sim, if not better! The art is amazing and the music is even better. There’s like 9 radio stations in the digger with their own rotation of custom songs (mostly about digging lol) and In world advertisements that play periodically (no real ads, just jokes). My personal favorites choice is DigFM.

Extras:
You can put on a hardhat in your warehouse and wear it into levels! 
You can play mini games like bowling or hook-a-duck with the digger!
You can customize the individual sections of your digger with paint, skins, and decals in the warehouse!

CONS or My problems with the game…:
I don’t have much here, but the damage system or grading system is a bit ehh.. I bumped into a grass hedge on the edge of the level with the arm and got points off? Not major but a bit stringent. Progression is a little slow. Not unbearable, but it does take a while before you get the other, larger excavators.

To the devs!!:
First of all, great job on a great game! I’ve already got my friend to get it too for multiplayer, but this brings up a couple of suggestions.
1: Multiplayer. Yes, there IS multiplayer, but you can only play with friends you can invite from the meta menu. If I had to change this, I’d say maybe add some sort of online hub where other people could join in larger public sandboxes or possibly actual multiplayer jobs?
2: Content. The game already has some Preem stuff, but possibly adding more vehicles that ran on the same system (Backhoe, SkidSteer, Cargo Crane) could really bring in more people! But I do also get why because the game is specifically “DIG vr”. Also, maybe some more customization like a bobble head in the cab, or the ability to customize the warehouse a bit?
3: Cleaning! Some people might hate this one, but, you already have the cleaning tools in the warehouse. Maybe after a couple of missions, dirt will build up, and you can optionally clean it off? Just a bit more realism that could be fun.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

The game is actually pretty good. The problem comes from when you hand it over to somebody else who instinctively click on "new game". Thereby erasing all your games and new excavators that you have unlocked unless there’s a way to get it back that I’ve had this has happen to me twice already. Very frustrating. ￼

This is as fine a digger game in VR as you could get. I’m only writing a review to mention to the developers that there is a spelling mistake in the game. In the tutorial Alignment is misspelled as Alignement. 

This is a fun game tho!

Best game ever you can literally dig holes for fun without your mom and dad yelling at you like this game like like multiplayer that’ll be really good I know it just came out but they had a multiplayer. I’m never stop playing it. It’s the best thing I’ve ever pre-ordered. I love it. It’s just a really good game. I recommend it to everybody that likes like construction tools. It’s fun. It’s a good game and you should play recommend it 100 out of 100

reviews.review_title: Simple dig game with lots in it
reviews.review_description:
If you like stuff like power wash sim you will like this game. I've not played multiplayer as I understand it, you can only play with people from your friends list.

Something a bit chill to do, there's different game. Modes and even mini games.

I don't like that there is only one save slot and you have to. Complete the whole level for it to save.

Not worth the price.  Can't open last 2 diggers.  Controls are sluggish.   Task are repetive.  Digging is frustrating specially when you have 98% of the task and can't get the remaining 2%   Graded missions are not forgiving and lost points for very little mistakes.  PLUS, wrote to WIRED PRODUCTIONS to know how to unlock the 2 big diggers and no answers.

Didn't play that much since I just got my q3, but tutorials and first 2 levels were a blast. Absolutely love the controls, I feel like I am driving it IRL. I feel confident that I can use an excavator if the Zombie apocalypse comes 😅
Don't waste time and money on this

I just tried this game and ended up finishing 4 jobs in one sitting. It is incredibly enjoyable to learn to actually be one with the machine and get to work with the tools properly. I am still with the mini digger but cannot wait to unlock the others. For those who enjoy playing games like Forza you will love this experience. Hands down to the amazing drum and bass radio that made me love the game even more!

I really like this game. However, considering it's called "Dig VR" you don't do all that much digging. You do a lot of lifting, moving, carrying, trimming, drilling, flattening, and just a little bit of digging. Controls are good, graphics are passable. Realistically 3.8 out of 5.

Could be a really fun game but geez I did not expect to get so motion sick so quickly. I have been unable to finish a job straight through due to dizziness and motion sickness. I have to break it up into small chunks. If you get VR sick, this is NOT the game for you. Even with all the comfortability settings on, I feel like I’m going to puke after max ten minutes. Unfortunately, this stops me from playing it because having to break every five minutes ruins the experience. I bet it’s great for people who don’t get motion sickness though. It is fun running the excavator.
I completed this whole game and every job was very fun. but now everytime i start the game my save is gone. but definitely a amazing game

reviews.review_title: Stay Away! HORRIBLE! STUCK IN TUTORIAL!
reviews.review_description: How do you get out of the d@3m tutorial that keeps restarting? I am so sick of restarting the tutorial, just to have to stop because of the barfyness, and when I try to go back, it wants to start the d@3m WAY-TOO -LONG tutorial over again!!! OMG, WHAT A PIECE OF CRAP!!! WHY WON'T THEY LET YOU OUT OF THE TUTORIAL???!!!
This game is great, hope to see you guys add in more fun modes or jobs

Controls are awful and feels disconnected, even with the updates and tips form devs.
If you're prone to motion sickness you won't last very long even with an ridiculous amount of vignetting in the comfort settings.

I played about 2-3 hours, and the tasks are more less just repetitive and the mini games weren't that exciting.

Too bad as I was very hyped from watching the trailer. 

Wouldn't recommend it to anyone, wish I could refund it because I won't play it ever again.
The game is amazing but there is a lack of updates. But I hope that when they port it to pc and psvr then they will add more to the game (multiplayer campaign).

The controls and tracking for the sticks on the machine are dookie. Several parts of the tutorial deliberately made me dock myself points.  The colors and contrast are very hard on the eyes and make it challenging to discern what certain things are. The settings menu is extremely basic and make it feel like your stuck in prison. This game could have been amazing and super fun, but they did awful on the execution.

This game really delivers on the whole driving one of these rigs. I have a few hours in the game, only the first two diggers open. I almost cannot stop playing this game. The controls allow you to position the machine very precisely. There's a large variety of addon tools for different tasks. This one is a must buy.

Dig VR is a cute simulator where you operate heavy machinery to dig, cut, and lift to complete various jobs.  There's only one problem: Videogame Logic!

You start out with an adorable "mini" digger to learn the controls and work your way to bigger models.  But there's one thing the mini digger has that none of the others have: 360-degree view potential because the engine and pumps are beneath you.  None of the diggers come with mirrors or even have backup cameras, and there's always a wheelbarrow or chair in some inconvenient spot for you to knock over!

As you progress you gradually feel more and more like every move you make could damage something, and the "Big" digger is the worst!  It's massive and terrifying.  You don't even have a "spotter" and you feel like a proverbial bull in a china shop because you can only see almost directly ahead of you and your boom length makes navigating turns all but impossible!

Fortunately, loosing points isn't that big a deal since you only need in-game currency for paint and decals for your diggers.

If you're a completionist with 0 patience: Would NOT recommend!

Keep in mind that this is a just game and NOT a training simulator.  And, as my dad used to say, "If the game isn't fun, why play it?"

On the other hand: If you want to simulate using a wrecking ball, there is a fun bowing minigame.

reviews.review_title: The game you wanted as a child
reviews.review_description: Fr tho best game 10/10, I grew up loving excavators, and bulldozers this is a game where you can do most of it. I wish I had all this when I was 8. A pled for the devs make a game that adds more gigantic machines.🙏🏽

Have installed uninstalled reinstalled played with control options right at the start when you start in the cab of the Excavator and it says to press a to continue nothing works. Could someone please help me out here? I don’t know how to get the game to start.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you're having difficulties. Please could you contact us at support@wiredproductions.com

reviews.review_description: Found the controls difficult to use, the digging bits not that fun, and I got bored halfway through the tutorial.

I like the game and it's mechanics, but i bought it mainly for career mode listed in description and I can't see any career here. Just a number of different levels to complete with storytelling about working as an excavator operator, but you manage nothing, you buy only skins, stickers and colors, every other tool or vehicle is provided by game when you need it. No repairs, shops, nothing i could even call a career. Also multiplayer has no coop, just some minigames to play. Worth mentioning if someone is thinking of buying the game for it's career mode or hoping to coop with friends on bigger worksites. There is nothing like that.

Best VR game out there probably, love driving around and it's taught me how to drive an irl digger. Would love to see a mini dumper be added where you can dump dirt into and press a button where you change into the dumper and drive down to the tarp for putting dirt if the tarps far away and could add more jobs with a tarp a bit far away

Seriously more updated more regularly. One of the best sitting games yet. And par to non on digger games! Just nailed it
Will continue to play when new uploads are added!!

Dig VR is a refreshing addition to the world of VR simulator games. So much fun with humour when you wouldn’t expect it. This game ticks so many boxes - it’s very entertaining and very enjoyable in a swivel chair. I love that you can customise your digger with paint and pattern combos and decals. Team cow!

I used to operate a backhoe for my business and this takes me back to those days. Trying to get the fluidity back working the controls and it is so much fun. The jobs are a blast to work, watching out for obstacles adds realism and enjoyment in completion. Just getting started and i find myself wanting to get back in to go to work when i actually get  home from work.  So much fun!

Love it!
Graphics, sound, physics… the in game radio!

This just feels great! One of the best Sim games on the Quest.

So smooth and the animations are perfect.
You have a tutorial, but quickly move on to the missions, starting off in a smaller digger and earning enough cash by doing missions, to buy bigger and better diggers.
When on jobs, you have to be careful to move and dig, without doing any or much damage to the stuff around you. Dig, smash and lift stuff to get your job done.

There’s plenty of customisation options… paints for various parts of your diggers, decals and skins.

You have a warehouse to purchase new diggers and equipment and also choose new jobs to take on.

You can also do time trials on each job to try beat records.

There is multiplayer in the game, where you can work with friends at jobs.

Game also has an in game radio with various stations.
Nice environments in the surrounding areas as the locals walk past and go about their daily stuff.
 
Highly recommend this to sim/digger enthusiasts

The game is nicely done. Graphics are simple but effective. All the cool attachments you could ever want, with well thought out missions/task structure. The controls are 1:1 what you would expect when in a excavator. My only gripe with the controls are they are just to touchy. You end up doing multiple inputs on accident. The game needs some built in dead zones that we can adjust to our liking. Other than that this game is great and I look forward to playing more.

reviews.review_title: 4.5 stars- Great game, w/ mild annoyance
reviews.review_description:
Starting with the things I LOVE:
The graphics, although cartoony, they are sharp and pretty.
-Gameplay is fun with plenty of tools and environments to work with, preventing the game from becoming monotonous and boring.

Things I like:
-The soundtrack is nice, although it's seemingly small. Can't really blame a game that's not centered around music, not having a bunch of songs for it. (It would be cool to have a "play your own music" options, but far from being an issue.

-The themes of the different locations are enjoyable and I like seeing them change as you progress. The music festival and Dinosaur bones being my favorite.

-Customization is a great factor that gives you something to work for, and the variety of cosmetics is vast. I guarantee there's something for everyone in the catalog.

Mini games are fun, which I'd add to the monotony prevention statement. Hardly anything I'd play for more that 20 minutes at a time, but sweet for when you still wanna play the game but don't want to jump into another level.

Problems ive come across:
-Blind spots in cramped areas have become an issue with the larger excavators. Although it adds to the realism of the game, it can be frustrating being in a tight spot and lightly bumping into an object resulting in lost points, but THEN having that object go flying in your only direct path out of said spot. You can't as much as touch it or move it which will result in double jeopardy and more lost points over a single mistake. (I had ALL S ratings until the medium excavator due to this) Mirrors or some way to get a wider view of your surroundings would make this a lot less frustrating.

-The hedge trimmers clip through the ground often, and you can't pull them above ground while the tool is attached. I unfortunately had to restart a level before I realized this can be resolved by detaching them which will pop it back above ground and reattaching to continue. I did lose points one time, though, due to it popping up and bumping a barrier.

OVERALL:
I have enjoyed 98% of the time I've played and have no regrets buying it. It's pretty incredible, relaxing, and fun. I'd say it's a lot more fun than the trailer let's on. There are some things that make it fall short of 5 stars in my opinion, but I'd also argue that 4-stars would be a harsh rating due to the factors that make this game great. This is the only game I've played since I got it, aside from maybe 10 minutes on the drums. I've put a lot of time into it so far and I'm still excited for after work each day where I can sit down and play for a bit.

surely made with a lot of love! fun gameplay and great(!) soundtrack:) 
worth your money
i love it🌳😌

If you want to feel like an excavator operator, this game is for you and not only you.

DigVR is a quite relaxing and soothing game. We sit comfortably in an armchair, start the game and operate excavators in career mode.

Operating the excavator is simple but requires a little practice because the levers are operated only with the wrists. Great mechanics, to be honest.

In addition, we have multiplayer and we can play with friends, e.g. bowling.

I recommend the game because it is really great and, as I said, quite relaxing.

reviews.review_title: Doesn't work with the RotoVR Explorer
reviews.review_description:
This is the only game that advertises working with the Roto VR chair but it doesn't work properly. When playing the game on a static chair the controls work fine. As soon as you sit on the chair and it starts rotating in space, the controls get all confused because you are physically changing plane with reference to your initial position. The result is as you turn the cab left, after a few degrees of turn, the shovel starts to go up as well. When you turn the cab right, the shovel starts to go down. The only way to keep the controls working is to recenter the view every few seconds, making the game unplayable on the RotoVR chair. Also when turning the cab in game, the rotation is very jerky on the chair even at the chair's lowest speed.
 UPDATE: The developper seems unwilling to accept that their game is flawed. They imply that I don't know how to operate the digger. NOTE TO DEVELOPPER: I do connect the game to the chair via the in-game Settings menu with the head dongle turned off. Please note that I can fully and easily operate the digger on a normal static chair. The problem arises when I am rotating in space on a RotoVR chair. I would suggest that you buy a chair and that you try it for yourself.

developer_response.body:
Please make sure you're connecting to the RotoVR Explorer via the connect button in the game's menu and not via the head dongle. DIG VR does not need/use the head dongle. 

Sorry to hear you are having issues with DIG VR while using the RotoVR Explorer. It sounds like you're experiencing issues with working the mechanics of the digger itself. The  physical controls will take a little time to master - I'd suggest making sure that you're 'twisting' your wrist and not pushing, as these will act very differently. It is also possible to control the digger entirely with stick controls.

If you need further help don't hesitate to contact us at support@wiredproductions.com

reviews.review_description: Game loads ok but my right hand is up in the air no idea why so it won’t let me click ok on the message you get when you first start the game there goes £14 does anyone have any advice on how to get around this please my boundary is set but my right hand is above the digger can and I can’t get it at all very disappointed

developer_response.body:
Hi there! The right hand appearing in the air may be as a result of your controller becoming disconnected, or because the battery is running low. Please ensure that both your controllers are connected, charged, working and you're in a well lit space before starting the game. 

If this does not resolve your issue, please reach out to us at support@wiredproductions.com where we will do our best to support you further - thank you!

reviews.review_description:
Since I first went on the search for an excavator game in VR I saw this on the coming soon. It's been months waiting for this if not a year at this point. But well worth the wait and the money. It has an arcade/fun style to it's scenery but not in a bad way. I love the details consistently spread through the background and sites you're on. Coming from the construction industry it's wonderful to see the developers taking the time to have safety signs and hardhats just to give you the full experience. 
The feel and controls do take a bit of learning and adjusting. Overall the controls really feel like the real thing, I've operated a few mini diggers in the real world and this is pretty close as you can get and could be a good test fit for those wanting to get into the Construction field. 
(Quick Tip: change digger controls to "arms only" as the "hands only" or "hands and arms" tend to make it too finicky)

I was concerned there wouldn't be any variety since after all you're just digging right? No, you dig, pick up posts, move barrels, cut down trees, auger holes, compress loose dirt. Thats just the career mode.
Then there are side games and quests where you can swing a bowling ball, hook rubber duckies and look for buried treasure. If this is the initial release, I can't imagine what else could come in future updates.

reviews.review_title: Does what it says on the box!
reviews.review_description:
Lots of fun to be had here! 

Can see this getting lots of DLC in the future (fingers crossed)!

Small improvement could be a little more realistic inertial physics. Example, it’d be cool if the vehicle rocked forward a bit after stopping suddenly. Other little things like that. 

Overall though a satisfying and fun little game!

reviews.review_title: Great sim but it could be better
reviews.review_description: Over all it’s a great game but there are a few  thing I would like to be added one is control of the blade on the mini excavators and throttle control on the excavators to make it more realistic control wise.

reviews.review_title: I'm stuck on the first level.
reviews.review_description:
Stuck on the garden level. When I drive to the highlighted circle to switch out the bucket for the compactor, after I connect the compactor, nothing happens. I can drive over to the site and compact the soil only to have points taken away from my score card. Rated as a 1 in hopes the devs would see this. The game is 5 stars, it's very fun. Best vr digger Sim on the market. Didn't want to wait for it to come out on pc so I picked up the quest version. Graphics are okay but understandable given the platform it's on. It would be cool to be able to listen to your own playlists. 

EDIT: The devs suggestion for me to try to fix the problem worked!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your thoughts with us. We're sorry to hear that your experience has not been as smooth as we would have hoped! 

We would suggest that, before driving to the compactor location, if you press 'A' to clear the email / message popup, the mission will progress. We're looking into this further, but believe that the issue occurs when the email is on screen and you reach the drive to location. 

To allow us to offer further support, could you provide a video or a detailed explanation of your experience to support@wiredproductions.com - thank you!

reviews.review_title: Realistic - a great way to learn !
reviews.review_description:
Only problem is that it won't save my progress - did a few missions and returned to menu, but the continue option is grayed out. 

Now excuse me while I go buy a mini digger....

developer_response.body:
Hi James, firstly we're glad to hear you're having a great time playing DIG VR and thank you so much for taking the time to leave a positive review despite experiencing some save issues. 

If possible, could you send us an email to support@wiredproductions.com so that we can help you further with this issue?

reviews.review_description: dig

reviews.review_title: you will really DIG this game
reviews.review_description: Really fun game, and a great use of VR! If you have ever wanted to diggers without getting your hands dirty, you can not go wrong!
I was supposed to by this game and how fun if is to play I am glad that I got this game
I drive diggers in real life; And this is just like it !
Loving this game, really enjoyable. Better than the real thing and with less damage. Hope there will be DLC later on. Please add a save option.

Been wanting a game like this for awhile and after playing, it’s awesome, great physics and gotta love the sandbox mode, just one suggestion I think we should be able to use every excavator in sandbox even if we haven’t unlocked them, even if we can buy them as a dlc

Playing on Quest 3 stand-alone and absolutely love this game!

Great graphics and gameplay, haven’t had any issues so far.

The hand tracking gets slightly wonky for certain machine heads, but easily overcome and doesn’t hinder gameplay.

Live the mini games too!

This game packs bundles of charm and character into what is a great Digger Sim.

With controls for both experts and beginners and a wealth of customisation options, you can find yourself lost inside the world for a while!

Plus, you can bring a friend along for the fun!!

Great cozy sim 👷🙌🏻

I don't give a lot of 5 star reviews as the is always something that can be Improved but Dig VR is the most fun I have had since smash drums and power wash in terms of just pick up & play mechanics that just work and your skill or judgement is what makes a difference. If you think this is going to be boring or repetitive then the is a small repetitive element to it but never boring. Worth the money and await some dlc hopefully.
The best game can someone invite me to a multiplayer

I’m a sucker for a simulator and I absolutely LOVE this one! 

So much to do and so well made, I love you can customise your digger and there are so many different attachments giving to a huge variety of tasks to complete for digging to even cutting down trees! 

The mini games are a superb addition, I mean who would have thought about using a digger to go bowling awesome! 

Love it if you don’t have it and you love a simulator like me! This one is on point and you need it!!

This game is everything a digger fan could hope for! The controls feel just like operating a real machine, making the experience incredibly realistic. The variety of attachments adds a whole new dimension, allowing you to do more than just digging, and the challenges keep things engaging. It’s absolutely worth the purchase—truly amazing. With plenty of levels and the chance to unlock different digger sizes, there’s always something new to enjoy.

"Dig VR takes one of your childhood dream jobs and makes it a reality in VR. Now you’re free to knock over fountains, chop through garden benches, and genuinely make a mess of things. Oh, and you can even do some digging too if you really want to!"

Read the full review over at The Elite Institute (dot) net!
This was worth the wait. A little bit of a learning curve. Spent time in the sandbox getting used to the controls. Lots of options for motion sensitivity. A real winner!

What a enjoyable game didn't think that a game like this would get me hooked but is so addictive you not only digging dirt but cutting down trees , cutting grass. Drilling holes etc.. just a great game
Everything your can imagine in a Vr excavator game sandbox,jobs,mini games and that you can customize your machines
Overall a very good game. The multiplayer is extremely fun, and so is the career. The diggers are extremely realistic in my opinion, and I just overall love it. 11/10
I got this game yesterday I cannot stop playing such a  good game
This is probably the best game ive played since into the radius
Its pretty good!

Cool addition to the VR space. Arcade like but good non the less, I've enjoyed my playtime so far. I used to do bit of work in 360 machines and this is probably the closest thing you will find to the real thing in VR you can't bank off or grade land it's just basically trenching atm when it comes to the digging side (don't know if that will change or not) you use digging buckets, grabbers, whacker plates , breakers, and cutting tools. It has a big verity of missions and arcade games to do to and a sandbox mode just for fun with the machine 
graphics aren't bad neither all be it there toony but it's nice. 
I recommend buying it.
GO DIG PPL💪
